questions.question: Which economic system dominated India on the eve of independence (in 1947)?
Which sector(s) employed the largest share of the working population in India on the eve of independence?
Which of the following instruments is primarily used by the RBI for monetary policy implementation in the post 1991 reform era?
A mixed economy involves both private and public ownership. What was a distinctive feature of India's mixed economy model?
questions.option_4: Public sector dominance in core industries

questions.question: As per Deloitte, how much will AI in healthcare boost India’s GDP by 2025?
Tariff reforms were central to the liberalisation of trade. What happened to import tariffs after 1991?
questions.option_4: It was replaced with import bans

questions.question: Which initiative is intended to encourage domestic manufacturing and attract foreign investment?
Privatisation under the 1991 reforms primarily involved:
questions.option_1: selling government shares in public sector enterprises

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of structural unemployment?
questions.option_1: A worker losing their job due to a recession
questions.option_2: A worker choosing not to work due to a lack of job opportunities
questions.option_3: A worker being unable to find a job due to discrimination
questions.option_4: A worker being unable to find a job due to a lack of skills in a new and expanding industry

questions.question: Special Economic Zones (SEZs) were established to promote export-oriented industrialisation. What is a primary incentive offered to firms operating in SEZs?
Which major Indian company was privatised in the 2000s under the Government of India’s disinvestment policy?
Tourism is classified as a tertiary activity because it involves:
India has been a World Trade Organization (WTO) member since _____.
When was the Monetary Policy Framework Agreement (MPFA) signed between the Government of India and the Reserve Bank?
Which institution played a key role in influencing the structural reforms of the industrial sector in India post 1991?
Why is infrastructure referred to as a 'universal intermediate'?
questions.option_1: It is the most profitable sector.
questions.option_2: It is used in the production of nearly all goods and services.
questions.option_3: It is universally available in every country.
questions.option_4: It connects urban and rural areas.

questions.question: Who is considered unemployed under the Usual Status (US) measure?
questions.option_1: Someone working only one day a week
questions.option_3: Someone not working but seeking work over a long period

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a tertiary activity?
Which of the following is considered a major social development outcome of India’s economic reforms since 1991?

Which of the following was among the first financial institutions in India to receive an 'in principle' approval from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to set up a bank in the private sector?
Which type of unemployment is most prevalent in Indian agriculture during the non-sowing season?
What is one key indicator of economic development that showed consistent improvement in India post 1991?
Which sector witnessed the slowest growth during the period of 1947 to 1991?
Which sector dominated India’s national income before independence?
